query: What is the current state of polling and political dynamics for Zambia's August 2026 presidential election, specifically Hakainde Hichilema's expected vote share? Include recent 2025-2026 polls, opposition coalition strength, whether Edgar Lungu/PF is running (note Lungu died in 2025), and turnout factors.
answer:
I've completed comprehensive research on Zambia's August 2026 presidential election. Here's the summary of key findings:

## Bottom Line
**Hichilema (UPND) is the clear frontrunner** heading into the 13 August 2026 election, but with meaningful uncertainty around undecided voters and turnout.

## Vote Share / Polling
- **ZERN academic survey** (representative, June–Nov 2025): Hichilema **55%**, opposition parties combined **13%**, undecided/refused **32%** — the decisive swing bloc.
- **Informal online polls**: 52–55% for Hichilema.
- **Polymarket**: ~**85.5%** probability the incumbent wins (as of 2026-07-07, trending up).
- One **outlier provincial survey** (May 2026, undisclosed methodology) claimed a collective opposition win — inconsistent with all other data.
- **2021 baseline**: HH won with 59%.

## Lungu / PF Status (critical development)
- **Edgar Lungu died 5 June 2025** — he had been the Tonse Alliance's presidential candidate.
- The Tonse Alliance replaced him with **Brian Mundubile (NRPUP)** at a January 2026 convention. Mundubile is gaining campaign momentum via ex-PF networks but faces a name-recognition/credibility gap.
- The PF proper is fractured by leadership disputes and alleged government interference.

## Opposition Strength & Turnout
- **Severe fragmentation**: 14 cleared candidates (26 filed); Lungu's death removed the one unifying figure. This lowers any challenger's odds of blocking HH's 50%+1.
- **Turnout**: projected >82% (vs ~70.9% in 2021); analysts flag **voter apathy / UPND base demobilization** as the single biggest risk to Hichilema.
- **Tilted playing field**: alleged gerrymandering, shortened voter-register inspection, Cyber Crimes Act arrests, and unequal campaign logistics favor the incumbent and fuel opposition legitimacy concerns.

The full structured findings (key facts, 15 sources, 12 quantitative data points) have been submitted.

sources.title: Tonse Alliance
sources.snippet: On 5 June 2025, the Tonse Alliance announced the death of their chairman, former President Edgar Lungu. In January 2026, a convention was held in which Brian Mundubile was selected as his replacement.

sources.title: Main findings from the Zambia Election Research Network Round 1 Survey
sources.snippet: 55% of respondents reported planning to vote for the president, 13% intended for an opposition party and 32% didn't know or refused.

sources.title: Elections 2026: is Zambia's democratic success story beginning to fray?
sources.snippet: 26 presidential candidates (14 approved), reflecting both an open electoral space and serious opposition fragmentation.

sources.title: Lungu's death and the perils of a weak opposition in Zambia
Zambia: The Challenges of Maintaining Democratic Progress
sources.snippet: The 59 percent of the vote he earned, galvanized by a strong youth turnout, was a significant breakthrough.

sources.title: Zambia Presidential Election Winner Predictions & Odds 2026
sources.snippet: Incumbent probability 0.855 as of 2026-07-07, up from 0.815 on 2026-06-30.

sources.title: 2026 Election Poll Survey Analysis
sources.snippet: UPND strongholds Southern 74%, Western 68%, North Western 66%; opposition collective poised for victory (outlier, methodology undisclosed).

sources.title: Hichilema dominates Mwamba online poll with clear 52% majority
HH wins 2026 presidential opinion poll among top six popular candidates
sources.snippet: Facebook poll projected Hichilema to secure 55 percent of the vote.

sources.title: Zambia scenario analysis predict over 82% voter turnout on 13th August

sources.snippet: ECZ certified 8.78 million voters; Lusaka Province over 1.43 million, Copperbelt 1.29 million.

sources.title: 2021 Zambian general election
sources.snippet: Hichilema defeated incumbent Edgar Lungu; Lungu conceded 16 August 2021.

sources.title: Mundubile: Energy, Momentum & the Credibility Gap
